name: dust.tt
description: Enterprise assistant platform exposing an API to build on its agents and connected data

provider.comment: Dust (dust.tt), Paris-based enterprise AI agent platform founded 2023 by Stanislas Polu and Gabriel Hubert.

category.comment: Enterprise assistant/agent platform; the API is a way to build on its agents and connected data rather than a raw model-inference API.

api-launched.comment: No clean GA date for the public v1 REST API; the platform emerged 2023 and the developer platform/API has been documented and available since 2024. Exact month not pinnable from primary sources (checked 2026-07).

status.comment: Developer platform is generally available and documented (OpenAPI 3.0, JS SDK, CLI).

api-docs.comment: Official API reference; OpenAPI & Postman collections at docs.dust.tt/reference/openapi.

auth-methods.comment: Bearer-token API keys (workspace-scoped, created in Build > Developer Tools > API Keys) plus OAuth2 access tokens; console access adds SSO (SAML/OIDC).

self-serve-signup.comment: Business plan is self-serve; a developer can sign up and mint an API key without sales contact (Enterprise remains custom/sales-led).

free-credits-trial.comment: 2026 pricing overhaul introduced a Free seat with 500 one-time credits; historically a 14-day free trial. Checked 2026-07.

regional-endpoints.comment: Data can be hosted in the EU or the US to meet regulatory needs.

data-residency-options.comment: Regional hosting (EU or US) offered as a data-residency guarantee, distinct from just multiple endpoints.

model-breadth.comment: Agents are model-agnostic across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, DeepSeek and xAI; the developer picks the model per agent.

open-weight-models.comment: Open-weight families are reachable (Mistral, DeepSeek) alongside proprietary models.

model-version-pinning.comment: Assistant platform: the underlying model is chosen in the agent configuration, not pinned per API request by the caller, so request-level snapshot pinning is not surfaced.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: No documented ability to deploy/call a customer's own fine-tuned or private model through the API; extensibility is via connected data, tools and MCP, not custom model hosting.

model-catalog-size.comment: Pricing/marketing cite 20+ models across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral and DeepSeek; borderline with 6-20 but stated as more than 20. Checked 2026-07.

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Modern multi-turn conversation/messages API (create conversation, post messages to an agent, stream the answer); it is agent-scoped, not a raw chat-completions endpoint.

streaming.comment: Real-time SSE event streaming of agent answers (streamAgentAnswerEvents).

tool-function-calling.comment: Assistant platform: tool use is configured on the agent (connected tools, MCP), not exposed as a request-level function-calling handshake the caller drives. See MCP Support.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Not applicable/undocumented: request-level function calling is not a surfaced primitive.

structured-output.comment: No server-enforced structured-output primitive (JSON mode / JSON schema) exposed; the API returns agent messages rather than a shaped-output field.

system-prompt.comment: Agent instructions are set in the agent configuration, not passed as a per-request system field, so a request-level system role is not surfaced.

image-input-vision.comment: Conversations support file attachments and the vision-capable underlying models (e.g. GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) can reason over images.

audio-input.comment: No documented audio/speech input primitive in the API.

document-pdf-input.comment: First-class document ingestion: file upload endpoints and data-source document upsert (RAG over PDFs and other documents).

image-generation.comment: Image generation is offered as an agent tool available through the platform/API.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: No documented speech/audio synthesis output through the API.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Embeddings are used internally for data-source retrieval/RAG but no standalone text-embeddings endpoint is exposed to callers.

prompt-caching.comment: Prompt/prefix caching is not a developer-facing feature of the Dust API; any caching by underlying providers is not surfaced or documented here.

batch-api.comment: No asynchronous batch/offline inference endpoint documented.

max-context-window.comment: Context depends on the underlying model selected in the agent (e.g. Gemini and Claude long-context models); Dust does not separately document a maximum context window, so not pinnable at check time (2026-07).

extended-reasoning.comment: Reasoning models (e.g. o3-mini) are selectable, but no API-level reasoning/thinking-budget control is exposed; reasoning behavior is a property of the chosen model.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party web search/browsing is available as an agent tool through the platform.

built-in-code-execution.comment: Code execution is offered as an agent tool (sandboxed function invocations stream via SSE).

mcp-support.comment: Both client-side and server-side MCP support; client-side MCP servers register, heartbeat, and stream tool requests over SSE.

files-retrieval-api.comment: File upload/download endpoints plus data sources with document upsert and managed retrieval (RAG) the agent can reference.

agents-assistants-api.comment: The core of the product: stateful agents and conversations API with managed state, tool orchestration, branching and scheduling.

openai-compatible-api.comment: Proprietary agent-oriented REST API (documented via OpenAPI); no OpenAI Chat-Completions-compatible base-URL swap.

official-sdks.comment: Official JavaScript/TypeScript client SDK (@dust-tt/client) plus a CLI (@dust-tt/dust-cli); no first-party Python SDK found.

native-api-style.comment: Proprietary agent/conversation-oriented REST API (workspace-scoped endpoints), documented with OpenAPI 3.0.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Webhook endpoints can trigger flows, with payload filtering and rate-limiting for scheduled automations.

published-sla.comment: SLAs are listed among Enterprise-plan controls (alongside SCIM, audit logs, dedicated support); specific uptime figure not published.

rate-limit-model.comment: Throughput/rate-limit model not clearly documented; usage is credit-metered (programmatic API billed at $0.01/credit) but per-tier throughput caps are not published.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Not a routing gateway; no documented automatic cross-provider failover. Model is selected per agent.

status-page.comment: Public Atlassian Statuspage (dust1.statuspage.io) tracking 13 components; incidents also surfaced in-app.

no-training-default.comment: Customer data is never used to train models, and no data is stored by third-party model providers (zero data retention by them).

zero-data-retention.comment: Zero data retention by underlying model providers; Enterprise admins can set custom conversation retention durations.

compliance-certifications.comment: SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant (EU data residency), and HIPAA compliant. ISO 27001 not confirmed on the security page (checked 2026-07).

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: SSO (SAML, OIDC) + SCIM provisioning, RBAC and Space-based access controls, plus audit logs with 365-day retention.

pricing-model.comment: Per-seat subscription (Pro/Max) metered in credits; a Free seat exists; programmatic/API usage billed at $0.01/credit. Checked 2026-07.

unified-billing.comment: One Dust account/credit balance covers all reachable models across providers.

cache-batch-discount.comment: No documented cached-prompt or batch-job pricing discount; billing is credit-based per model/task/tool usage.

cheapest-entry.comment: A Free seat with 500 one-time credits is the lowest-friction start; paid Business seats are then self-serve pay-as-you-go per seat. Checked 2026-07.
